editors_note.headline: This week
editors_note.thesis: We're watching OpenAI reveals more details about its agreement with the Pentagon — it's the one most likely to affect vendor and roadmap decisions this quarter.
editors_note.hook: We're flagging the lead for leadership — it's the one most likely to shift budgets or priorities.
editors_note.spotlight: The lead story is the most likely to change near-term decisions for teams shipping AI products.
editors_note.underhyped: Microsoft's Project Silica promises eternal storage. It can't get there from here deserves more attention than it's getting.
editors_note.risk: Keep an eye on Trump moves to ban Anthropic from the US government — it could shift budgets or vendor assumptions.

lead_story.title: OpenAI reveals more details about its agreement with the Pentagon
lead_story.what_happened: OpenAI is making moves. Check whether your vendor assumptions still hold.
lead_story.our_take: The signal is real; the timing matters more than the headline. Use it as a planning input (TechCrunch AI).
lead_story.what_to_do: Flag OpenAI for your leadership team — it may shift near-term priorities.

sections.title: Open-source picks
sections.deck: Open-source projects worth a closer look.

articles.title: CoPaw
articles.summary: CoPaw is a self-hosted AI assistant that runs locally or in the cloud, with integrations for DingTalk, Feishu, QQ, and Discord.
articles.what_happened: CoPaw ships a self-hosted AI assistant with multi-platform chat integrations (Discord, DingTalk, Feishu) and local or cloud deployment.
articles.why_it_matters: Self-hosted means your data stays on your infra — useful if you need an internal assistant without third-party API dependencies.
articles.what_to_do: Check CoPaw’s issue backlog and last commit date before depending on it for production use.
articles.our_take: The multi-platform angle is smart, but we’d want to see sustained maintainer activity before betting on it.

articles.title: Show HN: IssueScout – Find open source issues worth contributing to
articles.summary: IssueScout surfaces ‘good first issue’ labels across repos, enriched with community health scores and AI difficulty estimates for new contributors.
articles.what_happened: IssueScout launched as an open-source discovery platform that matches new contributors to welcoming repos using health scores and AI difficulty ratings.
articles.why_it_matters: New contributors waste hours on abandoned repos with mislabelled issues — IssueScout filters for active, well-maintained projects.
articles.what_to_do: Try IssueScout this week if you maintain an OSS project and want to attract first-time contributors.
articles.our_take: The AI difficulty estimate is the differentiator here — most ‘good first issue’ labels are unreliable without it.

articles.what_to_do: Add Microsoft's Project Silica to your weekly review and decide on a next step.
articles.our_take: Watch closely rather than act now — the picture will get clearer soon.

articles.title: Firefox 149 beta develops a split personality
articles.summary: Firefox is getting traction for a reason. Skim the details before your next sprint (via The Register).
articles.what_happened: Firefox update. Not urgent, but worth knowing about.
articles.why_it_matters: Firefox sounds minor now, but it could change your shortlist by next quarter.
articles.what_to_do: Spend 15 minutes on Firefox — decide if it warrants a deeper look or can be shelved.
articles.our_take: Worth a quick check against your stack — Firefox could affect cost or scale assumptions.

articles.title: Iran all but vanishes from the global internet amid US-Israel strikes
articles.summary: Quick update on Iran — relevant if you're evaluating tools in this space (via The Register).
articles.what_happened: Brief Iran roundup — relevant if you work in this area.
articles.why_it_matters: Keep an eye on Iran — small moves here tend to become bigger ones.
articles.what_to_do: Review Iran and decide on one concrete follow-up action by end of week.
articles.our_take: Don't let FOMO drive architecture. Validate on your own terms before changing course.

learn_this_week.title: Machine Learning Specialization
learn_this_week.note: Andrew Ng's refreshed ML curriculum. The gold standard intro. Audit free, cert paid.

prompt_of_the_week.text: Generate a week's worth of social media posts about [topic] in the voice of a technical thought leader — concise, opinionated, no fluff.
prompt_of_the_week.why: Technical audiences punish generic content. Specifying 'opinionated, no fluff' constrains the model away from safe, bland takes — the output reads more like a real practitioner's voice.
prompt_of_the_week.tip: Replace [topic] with your area. Add 2-3 example posts you admire for tone matching.
